text: The XCP Flight Software shall process science data from the camera.
The XCP Flight Software shall relay science data to the Spacecraft Control Unit in the form of CCSDS Source Packets.
The XCP Flight Software shall receive commands from the Spacecraft Control Unit that establish the current instrument state and camera mode.
The XCP Flight Software shall transmit detailed housekeeping data to the Spacecraft Control Unit in the form of CCSDS Source Packets.
The XCP Flight Software shall receive a time message from the Spacecraft Control Unit.
The XCP Flight Software shall synchronize the XCP local copy of the spacecraft clock using the received time message.
The XCP Flight Software shall control the heaters on the telescope tube.
The XCP Flight Software shall control the heaters on the thermal baffles.
The XCP Flight Software shall read data from the Telescope Alignment Monitor.
The XCP Flight Software shall maintain primary and alternate configurations in EEPROM.
The XCP Flight Software shall autonomously switch to the alternate configuration if the primary configuration fails to boot.
The XCP Flight Software shall include a Memory Scrubber task to repair single bit errors in DRAM.
The XCP Flight Software shall reset the processor via the watchdog timer upon detection of multiple bit errors.
The XCP Flight Software shall report errors in the housekeeping telemetry.
The XCP Flight Software shall transmit a heartbeat message to the Spacecraft Control Unit to indicate instrument aliveness.
The XCP Flight Software shall transition to the RED state upon receipt of a commanded transition from the MANUAL state.
The XCP Flight Software shall transition from the RED state to the MANUAL state after the execution of a RED command.
The XCP Flight Software shall reject any non-RED command received while in the RED state.
The XCP Flight Software shall support Automatic, "Preplanned", and Target of Opportunity observation sequences.
The XCP Flight Software shall perform a centroid calculation on the image when a source is detected.
The XCP Flight Software shall transmit an XRT Centroid Error message if the centroid calculation fails.
The XCP Flight Software shall transmit an XRT Position message if the centroid calculation is successful.
The XCP Flight Software shall switch from fast timing mode to normal timing mode when the lit pixel rate falls below 40 cps.
The XCP Flight Software shall switch to photon counting mode when the pixel rate falls below 2 cps.
The XCP Flight Software shall perform Built-In Test Stage 1 during the boot process.
The XCP Flight Software shall perform Built-In Test Stage 2 during the initialization process.
The XCP Flight Software shall provide an application interface to the MIL-STD-1553B data bus hardware.
The XCP Flight Software shall provide an application interface to the RS-422 hardware interface.
The XCP Flight Software shall provide an application interface to the Analog/Digital and Digital/Analog Converters.
The XCP Flight Software shall provide a file system compatible with the Microsoft Disk Operating System.
The XCP Flight Software shall manage communications with the SCU over the 1553 interface at the application data protocol level.
The XCP Flight Software shall be capable of loading a sequencer program from the EEPROM File System.
The XCP Flight Software shall use the VxWorks 5.3 real-time multi-tasking operating system.
The XCP Flight Software shall restrict real-time HK packets to a maximum size of 230 bytes.
The XCP Flight Software must not segment any packet that requires processing by the ITOS ground system.
The XCP Flight Software must not compress any packet that requires processing by the ITOS ground system.
The XCP Flight Software should be allocated at least 10MB of memory for observation buffers to meet the science telemetry rate.
The XCP Flight Software shall be developed in accordance with the Software Engineering Department Standard Software Process.
